def: s.f. Deep thought, reflection, meditation, consideration, deliberation, deep research; abstraction; close attention or care; — name of a province or region in Arabia
more: n. f. 1. Deep thought or reflection; abstraction (بچار 2, 5). 2. Close attention or care. gaur pardākht . Bringing up; maintenance. gaur se dekhnā . To observe minutely; consider attentively. gaur-talab , Worth consideration. gaur-i-kāmil . Full consideration; mature deliberation. gaur-kardah , adj. Deliberate; prepense. gaur kar-ke, baṛe gaur se, gaur o tāammul se , adv. Deliberately; advisedly. gaur k. , v. a. 1. See بچارک 1-3, 5, 6. 2. To incline towards; countenance. Gangā, kar gaur garīban pe! bin gaur garīb kahā kareṅge? O Ganges, goddess! look upon the poor! without thy help what shall the poor do? be-gaurā, be-gaurī , adv. Carelessly.

s. f. Reflection, consideration, deliberation, meditation, deep thought. غورکرنا g̠ẖaur karnā, To consider, reflect, to investigate, to fathom by consideration or investigation. غور پرداخت g̠ẖaur-pardāḵẖt, or غوروپرداخت g̠ẖaur-o-pardāḵẖt, s. f. Attendance on, attention to. ¶ g̠ẖaur, n. prop. A country or province in Arabia.
subs: or g̠aur-o-pardāḵẖt, s.f. Attendance (on, -kī), attention (to); bringing up; maintenance
s.f. The looking closely (into, -kī), minutely regarding or examining, thoughtful consideration (of), deep research (into)
adv. Thoughtfully; attentively; with, or after, due consideration, deliberately, advisedly
(-par, -ko, or -kī), To reflect (on), to consider thoughtfully, meditate (on); to investigate, to fathom by consideration or investigation; to regard with attention, to look (upon).
ex: اس فیصلے پر ہم نے کافی غورکیا۔
is faisle par hamne kāfī ġaur kiyā.
We put enough thought into this decision.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غور (ġawr), from Arabic غَوْر (ḡawr, “bottom, depth, (fig.) deepness in thought”).

def: adj. (f. -ī), White, pale; of fair complexion, fair; — yellow; — red, pale red; — s.m. A kind of buffalo, the Bos gaurus; — white mustard; the seed of white mustard (used as a weight); — the moon; — the plant Grislea tomentosa; — the filament of a lotus; — saffron.
more: n. f. A Hin. marriage ceremony in which the future bride is worshipped by her near relatives.
n. m. S. गौड 1. An inhabitant of Gauṛ , the ancient capital of Bengal. 2. A caste of Brahmins.
adj. Fair-complexioned; s. f. A name of Pārvatī.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گور (gōr), from Middle Persian gpl, gwbl (gabr, “hollow, cavity, womb”).

def: s.f. 'The brilliant goddess, a name of the goddess Pārvatī; — a (Hindū) marriage ceremony in which a girl who is about to become a bride is worshipped by her near relatives.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گور (gōr), from Middle Persian gpl, gwbl (gabr, “hollow, cavity, womb”).

def: s.m. Name of a country, 'the district of Gaur,' the central part of Bengal extending from Bang to the borders of Orissa); — the ancient city of Gaur (formerly the capital of Bengal); — the inhabitants (or an inhabitant) of Gaur; — a caste of Brāhmaṇs; an individual of that caste
subs: s.m. One of the ten tribes of Brāhmaṇs
s.m. An important tribe of Brahminical descent in the north-west of India
s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts in the district of Farrukhābād
s.m. The country of Gauṛ, or of the Gaur Brāhmaṇs
s.m. Name of a class of Rājpūts
s.m. One of the twelve divisions of the Kāyath caste.

def: s.f. = gaurī, 'the goddess Pārvatī.'
more: n. f. lit . fair-complexioned. The goddess Pārbatī , the wife of Mahādeo .
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑁄𑀭 (gora), from Sanskrit गौर (gaurá).

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Ahīrs; — (dialec.) hail.

def: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode; — name of a caste of Hindūs in Orissa (who generally serve as palanquin-bearers in Bengal; and as 'bearers' or servants who take charge of the wardrobe, bedding, &c.).
more: s. m. 1. Name of a cast in Orissa, who are generally pālkī carriers and bearers. 2. Name of a rāgiṇī or musical mode.

def: s.m. Name of an obscure tribe of Rājpūts on the borders of the Aligarh district.

s.m. Weight, heaviness; gravity, importance; high value or estimation; dignity, respect, venerableness; respectability; grandeur; repute.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गौरव (gaurava, “cumbrousness, importance”); see गौर (gaura, “shining, brilliant”) for more.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Highly esteemed or valued, venerable, respectable, reputable; important; — a highly esteemed or venerable person.

s.f. 'The brilliant goddess,' name of the goddess Pārvatī (the wife of Śiva); — a young girl (prior to menstruation, or of about ten years of age), a maiden; — the vigils and festivities of Bhādra-pad-śukl-paksh in honour of Pārvatī; — name of a rāginī or musical mode
more: n. f. S. गौरी 1. A rāgnī or musical mode. 2. The goddess Pārbatī .

def: s.f. Rum or spirit distilled from guṛ or molasses; — name of a rāginī or musical mode.
more: s. f. Name of a rāgiṇī or musical mode. See گوري gaurī.

def: s.m. An inhabitant of Gaur; (hence) the followers (or a follower) of Ćaitanya, a Brāhmaṇ philosopher of Nadiyā (who flourished about the year 1407 of Sālivāhan).
more: s. m. Inhabitant of Gauṛ; hence, the followers of Chaitanya of Nadiyā are so called. This philosopher flourished about the year 1407 of Sālivāhana. He was by birth a Brāhmaṇ; and, having become a Sannyāsī, maintained that the doctrines of the Vedas had been hitherto misunderstood, and explained them in a manner peculiar to himself. His sect is a branch of the Vaish̤ṇava or worshippers of Vish̤ṇu. His disciples in various parts of India are said to amount to five millions. His followers believe him to have been an incarnation of the deity.

def: s.f. A sparrow (syn. gargaiyā).
more: Sant. n. f. 1. ( gargaiyā ) A hen-sparrow. 2. A huqqā made of clay.

def: s.m. Name of an inferior tribe of Rājpūts in Agra and Mathurā.
s.m. (orig.) 'A cry for aid or succour'; a title of Moḥammadan saints (whose ardour of devotion, according to vulgar tradition, is such, that in the act of worship their head and limbs fall asunder).
more: n. m. A title of Mah. saints. Their ardour of devotion, according to vulgar tradition, is such that in the act of worship their head and limbs fall asunder.
s. m. A title of Muhammadan saints, whose ardour of devotion, according to vulgar tradition, is such, that in the act of worship their head and limbs fall asunder.

Name of a Vedic metre of twenty-four syllables (generally arranged as a triplet of three divisions of eight syllables each); a hymn composed in this metre; — a sacred verse from the Rig-veda to be recited mentally by every Brāhman at his morning and evening devotions (the Gāyatrī verse is personified as a goddess, the wife of Brahmā, and mother of the four Vedas, and also of the first three classes of Hindūs in their capacity of twice-born: there is but one Gāyatrī of the Vedas; but according to the system of the Tāntrikas there are a number of mystical verses called by that name, and each deity has one in particular: the repetition of the Gāyatrī is considered necessary to salvation).
s.m. (f. -iṇī), One who sings hymns; a singer; — the tree Acacia catechu.
more: n. f. A sacred verse from the Rig Veda repeated mentally.

s. m. Red colour with which women paint the face: see اپٿن or غازه کاري .اپٿنا g̠ẖāza-kārī, s. f. Painting the face with a red colour.
subs: s.f. Applying rouge to the face, &c.

ety: inf. n. of غزو 'to go forth to war'
def: s.m. Waging war, going forth to war (esp. against infldels)
more: s. m. Making war (against infidels). غزاصفا g̠ẖazā ṣafā, War, plunder, devastation.

ety: imperf. part. of gazīdan = prep. ga + zīdan = zadan; — Zend vi+rt. jan; S. वि+हन् (हन्ति)
def: part. adj. Biting; stinging; injuring, hurting; hurtful; — s.m. Injury, hurt; loss, damage.
more: 1. part. act. (of گزيدن) Biting, hurting. 2. s. Loss, hurt.

ety: inf. n. of غضب 'to be angry'
def: s.m. Anger, passion, wrath, rage, fury, vengeance; wrong, impropriety, injustice, oppression, outrage; violence, force, compulsion; — a curse, calamity, affliction, woe, a fearful thing, an awful event; — adj. & s.m. Angry, wrathful, offended, &c.; fearless, daring; outrageous; great, excessive, intense, tremendous, awful, fearful, dreadful, shocking; woeful, calamitous, injurious, hurtful, detrimental (to, -ke ḥaq-meṅ); — too good; very fine, splendid, dazzling, very beautiful; wonderful, rare, extraordinary, unique; — an oppressor, &c. ( = g̠aẓabī); a fearless man, a dare-devil
more: adj. غضب was angry. 1. Angry; offended; displeased. 2. Too good; too bad; excessive; dreadful; shocking. Gazab garmī paṛtī hai . 3. Gigantic; stupendous; splendid; dazzling. Āj to gazab ban-ke nikle ho! 4. Very beautiful; angelic. Ham jis pe haiṅ āshaq, woh parī-zād gazab hai . Zauq. 5. Unique ( achambā , and anokhā 1-5). Uskī jo bāt hai, woh gazab hai . 6. Destructive; hurtful; baneful. Yeh bad-mizājī tumhāre haq meṅ gazab hai .

n. m. Vengeance; wrath; woe (اَفت). 1. Buṛhyā gazab kī puṛyā . Prov. An old woman is a packet of evil (Pandora's box). 2. Burā hākim Khudā kā gazab . Prov. A bad ruler is an instrument of God's vengeance. 3. Yeh kyā thoṛā gazab hai ki Hindustān ko kaṅgāl kar dīyā? Is the impoverishment of India a small calamity? 4. Dārūë gazab khamoshī . Prov. Wrath's cure is silence. 5. Khudā ke gazab se zarā dil meṅ kāṅp, Chugal-khor ke muṅh ko ḍaste haiṅ sāṅp . Mīr Hasan. With fear of wrath divine your heart should tremble just, The wicked tongues' of tale-bearers the snake bite must. gazab-i-ilahī, yā /?/khudā . Divine wrath, or gazab toṛnā, yā ḍhānā , Wom. v. a. To wreak one's vengeance on. Mue gazbī ne gazab ḍhā rakkhā hai . Mah. Wom. gazab ṭūṭnā , v. n. See āfat ānā , 1. gazab k. , v. a. 1. (Rus. gajab ghālnā ) To do or work wonders; to surprise. Bolte tum to kyā gazab karte, Sau sitam karte ho, b…

1. s. m. Violence, oppression, injustice, compulsion, passion, rage, fury, wrath, vengeance, anger, curse, calamity. 2. adj. Angry. غضب آلوده g̠ẖaẓab-ālūda, adj. Oppressive. غضب کرنا g̠ẖaẓab karnā, To oppress, to tyrannise (over). غضبناک g̠ẖaẓab-nāk, adj. Enraged, indignant, wrathful. غضبناکي g̠ẖaẓab-nāk-ī, s. f. Indignation.
subs: adj. Full of rage, enraged, wrathful, indignant; oppressive, &c.
or g̠aẓab ṭūṭnā (-par), To be visited by (the) wrath (of God), to be overtaken by calamity or affliction
or g̠aẓab ḍhānā (-par), To visit (one) with wrath, to wreak vengeance (on); to practise cruel oppression (on), &c.
'The wrath of God'; — have some fear of God's wrath! good gracious! how shocking! how fearful! (a phrase used when one sees or hears another saying or doing something outrageous or revolting)

(rustic, gajab ghālnā), v.n. To be angry or wrathful (with, -par), be enraged or indignant (at); to practise oppression (on), to oppress, to tyrannize (over); to commit an impropriety, or an outrage; to work or do harm or mischief; to do anything fearful or awful; — to do something wonderful or surprising, to work wonders
adj. Full of rage, enraged, indignant, passionate, furious
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غضب (ġazab), from Arabic غَضَب (ḡaḍab).

def: adj. & s.m. (H. fem. g̠aẓaban), Angry, wrathful, passionate, furious; — fearless, &c. (see g̠aẓab); — a passionate, or a violent man; an oppressor; a fearless man, a dare-devil, a terrible fellow; a firebrand; — s.f. Anger, wrath, &c. ( = g̠aẓab).
more: n. m. gazban, gajban , n. f. Wom. An oppressor; a firebrand. 1. Baṛā gazbī marduā hai . Wom. 2. Yo ke kām kare sai gajban! sai yo kām burā, kāman . W. Rus.

ety: gaz˚, prob. fr. gazīdan+dim. aff. ak
def: s.f. A relish (as fruit, or sweets, taken with wine, or after opium, &c.); a sweetmeat made of sesamum and sugar.
more: n. f. 1. Relish (taken after wine, opium, etc.). 1. Kaho to mohaiyā ho bazm-i-sharāb. Maṅgāëṅ gazak ke liye bhī kabāb! Shauq. 2. Afyūnī kī gazak miṭhāī, Sharābī kī gazak namkīnī . Prov. For opium-eaters sweets, for wine-bibbers salted food. 2. A sweetmeat of sesamum and sugar. 1. Gajak khoprā jāṛe kā! Delhi Street cry. 2. Gazak, paṭṭī, halvāson jāṛe kā! Delhi Street cry.
s. f. A relish (eaten with wine, &c.), a devil.

ety: inf. n. of غزل 'to talk, &c. in an amatory and enticing manner'
def: s.f. An amatory poem, an ode
more: n. f. pl. gazalyāt . An ode; an amatory or Bacchanalian poem. gazal-khuāṅ . A reciter of odes. gazal-khuānī , n. f. The reciting of odes. gazal kahnā , v. n. To compose an ode.

s. f. An ode, an amatory poem. (Plur. غزليات). غزل خوان g̠ẖazal-ḵẖẉān, A reciter of odes. غزل خواني g̠ẖazal-ḵẖẉān-ī, s. f. The reciting of odes. غزل سرا g̠ẖazal-sarā, adj. Singing an ode. غزل سراي g̠ẖazal-sarā-ī, s. f. Singing of odes.
subs: adj. & s.m. Singing, or singer of, an ode
s.f. The singing of an ode or odes
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَزَل (ġazal), from Arabic غَزَل (ḡazal).

def: s.m. A young gazelle or deer, a fawn; — the sun; — a delicate young person.
more: (improp. g̠ẖizāl ), s. m. 1. A young deer, a fawn. 2. The sun. 3. A delicate young person. (Pers. plur. غزالان).

ety: for A. غزالة, fem. of g̠azāl
def: s.m. A young gazelle; a young deer, a fawn.
more: (improp. g̠ẖizāla ) s. m. A young deer, a fawn.

def: part. adj. = gazā, q.v. s.m. Rouge for the face; perfumed powder for the hair or skin (cf. upṭan)
subs: s.f. Applying rouge to the face, &c.

r: gazand, vazañt, ga, zandan, zadan, gazā

more: s. f. m. Misfortune, calamity, loss, injury, mischief, harm.

ety: imperf. part. of gazīdan; see gazā; — ˚anda = Zend añṭ = S. ant
def: part. adj. & s.m. Biting: stinging; injurious, hurtful; venomous, noxious; — a biter; stinger; — a venomous reptile, &c.

def: s.f. Biting; stinging; bite, sting; — mordacity; the power to hurt or harm.
s. f. Biting, stinging.

ety: v.n. fr. غضفر 'to be or become heavy or sluggish'
def: s.m. A big-bodied, or burly man, a champion, hero; — a lion.
more: s. m. A lion, a powerful man, a hero.

def: ( = S. गर्जर), s.m.(?) A carrot (syn. gājar, q.v.).
more: n. m. 1. A passage; ingress and egress; access. 2. A road; way. 3. Escape. 4. A jurisdiction. Guzar-i-faiz bāzār .
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گزر (gazar). Doublet of گاجر (gājar).

def: s.m. (Dakh.) = kazh-dum, q.v.
s.f. A coarse kind of (cotton) cloth.
more: n. f. A thin coarse cloth (of cotton). gazī gāṛhā, moṭā jhoṭā , n. m. Inferior coarse cloth. gazī gāṛhā pahannā, juār bājrah khānā , v. n. To live as a poor man.
s. f. A coarse kind of cloth (of cotton).

ety: for A. غازٍ g̠āziṉ, act. part. of غزو 'to go forth to fight with an enemy'
def: s.m. One who engages in a warring expedition; one who fights against infidels; a warrior, a conqueror, a hero
more: n. m. A Mah. who is victorious over infidels. Mare to shahīd, māre to gāzī . Prov. A martyr if killed, a Gāzi if he kills. gāzī-mard , n. m. 1. A hero. 2. ( Met .) A horse. gāzī-mīyāṅ , n. m. 1. The nephew of Sultān Mahmūd who is worshipped as a saint. Thoṛā kareṅ Gāzī mīyāṅ, bahut kareṅ ḍafālī . Prov. The Gazi's war feats small the drummers loudly call. 2. (— kī chhaṛyāṅ ) A festival held in commemoration of him.

(part. act. of غزا or غزو To purpose, to intend, to undertake a military expedition. Heb. To be strong or brave) s. m. A conqueror, a hero, one who fights against infidels. (Pers. plur. غازيان). غازي مرد g̠ẖāzī-mard, A hero; ( met. ) a horse. غازي ميان g̠ẖāzī-miyāṅ, was nephew of Sult̤ān Maḥmūd G̱ẖaznawī; a festival is held in commemoration of him at the beginning of May.
subs: s.m. A man of war, a hero; (met.) a horse
s.m. A name of the nephew of Sultān Maḥmūd (he is worshipped as a saint); — g̠āzī-miyāṅ, or g̠āzī-miyān kī ćhaṛiyāṅ, A festival held in commemoration of Gāzī-miyāṅ (at the beginning of May).

def: part. Bitten; stung; injured, hurt; alarmed.

ety: ga+zīdan = zadan; Zend vi+rt. jan; S. वि+हन्
def: v.t. & s.m. (gerund) To bite; sting; to injure; — biting; stinging, &c.
more: inf. or verb. n. Biting, to bite.

def: adj. To be bitten.
s.m. A thick (or close) and rather coarse kind of cotton cloth.
more: s. m. A thick (or close) and rather coarse kind of cotton cloth.

def: s.m. A superintendent of the revenue; a person who collects the taxes of a town and pays them to the taḥṣīl-dār; a manager, an agent; — a commander; a hero; champion; — the night-watch.

more: s. (for قوةغازية) The faculty of intending or of purposing.

def: adv. Like a warrior, bravely, heroically.

s.m. = گاذر gāẕur, q.v. s.m. A washerman (syn. dhobī).
war, battle, clash; the battles in which Prophet Muhammad (SWT) was involved in

more: s. m. Name of a bird.

ety: S. अकृतः or कन्द+कः; cf. geṅd, geṅdā

more: n. m. 1. An unfledged bird; a nestling. 2. ( Met .) An infant. 3. Lenitive or aperient food.
s. m. 1. A young unfledged bird, a nestling. 2. ( met. ) An infant.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Simple, silly, soft, weak-minded; — a simpleton, an imbecile (syn. bodlā).
more: adj. Simple; silly; soft; weak. geglā , n. m. 1. A slattern; slut; trollop. 2. A simpleton. geglā-pan , n. m. Simplicity.

def: s.m. Simplicity, &c. (see geglā).
s.f. A simple woman; a stupid woman; a trollop, a slattern (syn. bodlī).
s.m. (dialec.) A crab ( = kekṛā, q.v.).
s.m. A house, dwelling, &c. (see gr̤iha).
more: s. m. A house, a dwelling.

def: wheat
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑁄𑀳𑀽𑀫 (gohūma), from Sanskrit गोधूम (godhū́ma, “wheat”). Doublet of گَنْدُم (gandum).

def: adj. Wheat-coloured; tawny, swarthy; — s.m. The colour of wheat; — a kind of grass.
more: 1. s. m. The colour of wheat. 2. Name of a grass. 3. adj. Of the colour of wheat, swarthy.

def: s.m. Wheat ( = gohūṅ).

n. S. गोधूम Wheat. 1. Khāūṅ to gehūṅ, na to rahūṅ ehūṅ . Prov. If I eat it shall be wheat, or else no food I'll touch. 2. Bijhṛā kī pīsan-hārī, gehūṅ ke gīt gāveṅ . Prov. She grinds coarse grains and sings the song for wheat! (She talks very big). 3. Lāl gehūṅ . Brown wheat. 4. Gehūāṅ raṅg . Wheat color. 5. Gehūṅ kī bāl nahīṅ dekhī . Not seen even the ear of wheat. (Inexperienced).
adj. & s.m. = gehūʼāṅ.
adj. (f. -ī) = geglā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Simple, silly, soft, weak-minded; — a simpleton, an imbecile (syn. bodlā).
more: adj. Simple. ¶ gīlā, 1. adj. Moist, damp, wet. 2. s. m. Name of a wild creeper. (Mimosa scandens).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀕𑁆𑀭𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*grilla, “wet”). Compare Punjabi گِلّا (gillā) / ਗਿੱਲਾ (gillā).

more: n. f. S. गन्दक or गेण्डु Pālī, geṅḍuko . 1. A ball (to play with). 2. ( Met .) A woman's breasts. geṅd ballā , n. m. Bat and ball; cricket. geṅd pheṅknā , v. n. To throw a ball; to bowl. geṅd-taṛī , n. f. Striking a ball at each other. geṅd khelnā , v. n. To play rounders. geṅd-ghar , n. m. A racket Court.
s. f. 1. A ball (to play with). گيند تڙي کهيلنا gend-taṛī khelnā, To play at striking a ball. 2. (dakh.) A marigold: see گيندا gendā.

def: tennis court; racquet court
wety: From گیند (gend, “ball”) + خانہ (xāna, “place”).

more: n. m. The Indian marigold; tagetes erecta , Wat. Mahabbat kī nahīṅ geṅde meṅ bū hai, Isī bāis se geṅdā zard-rū hai. geṅdaī , adj. Yellow like the marigold.
n. m. S. गण्ड Pālī, ganḍako . A rhinoceros.

ex: گینڈے کی کھال ہے اس کی۔
He has the hide of a rhinoceros (i.e., it is very difficult to upset him).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गण्डक (gaṇḍaka, “rhinoceros”).

def: adj. Of the colour of the marigold; — s.f. The colour of the marigold, yellow colour.

s.f. A coil, &c. ( = kunḍlī, q.v.). s.f. = kundrī, q.v. s.f. (dialec.), A kind of vegetable, Bryonia grandis; — a whirlwind (syn. bagulā; bawanḍar).

s.f. = next.

v.t. To throw (as in wrestling, &c.), to overcome, to throw down; to throw about, to strew, scatter; to put (into), to apply (as antimony to the eyes); to prepare, make (as pickle, &c.)

To throw one; overcome. Nīche gernā . 3. To strew; scatter. 4. To apply antimony, etc. to the eyes. 5. To prepare; make. Nībū kā āchār ger do!

def: s.m. Red earth, soil of a red colour; red ochre, red chalk; brick-dust (of writing-boards); ruddle (syn. gil-ě-armanī).

The red blood from their ebon bodies looked as bright, As flows red ochre from black mountain 'fore the sight. ger'vā, geruā , adj. Of the color of red ochre. ☞ Clothes of this colour are worn by Hin. religious mendicants and by prisoners in jails.
s. m. A kind of red earth or ochre.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Of the colour of red ochre; red; coloured or dyed with red ochre; covered with red ochre.

s.m. The mountain partridge; a kind of pheasant.

def: s.m. The grey horn-bill, Meniceros bicornis; also Buceros malabaricus (syn. ghan-ćiṛī, q.v.s.v. ghan).
more: n. m. The small grey horn-bill; meniceros bicornis . It is found over most parts of the plains of India except the extreme north. It breeds in April, May, and June in holes of decayed trees, eggs white. Its color is a uniform grey, with long tail and large bill.
